requirements_conformance.evidence: GeminiClient(LLMClient) implements complete(prompt, *, model, temperature, max_tokens, timeout) -> str matching agents/llm/client.py's abstract signature exactly. Correctly POSTs to /models/{model}:generateContent, maps prompt to contents[0].parts[0].text, parses candidates[0].content.parts[0].text. Satisfies issue #20's 4.1.2 acceptance criteria.

architecture_conformance.evidence: Matches OpenRouterClient's established pattern: same constructor kwarg shape (api_key/base_url/model/timeout/transport), same env-var-fallback-then-raise-at-construction pattern (GEMINI_API_KEY), same httpx.Client+MockTransport injection point, same try/except httpx.HTTPError -> ProviderClientError(LLMError) wrapping, same JSON-shape defensive parsing style. No provider SDK dependency added (deliberate REST-via-httpx choice, disclosed in module docstring, consistent with sibling clients).

regression_risk.evidence: agents/llm/ suite: 52 passed, 0 failed (includes gemini, openrouter, ollama, client interface tests). Full repo suite (python3 -m pytest .) fails at collection in ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py due to a pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime version mismatch (gencode 6.33.5 vs runtime 5.29.6) unrelated to this change -- confirmed pre-existing via git log (introduced by 9794fd8, agents/ingestion/, no relation to agents/llm/). This commit touches only new files agents/llm/gemini_client.py and agents/llm/test_gemini_client.py; no existing file modified, so it cannot be the cause of the ingestion collection error.

edge_cases.evidence: Tests cover: missing API key at construction, malformed/non-JSON response, missing 'candidates' key, empty candidates list, missing content.parts.text, HTTP 5xx, connection error, model override per-call, omitted max_tokens. All raise GeminiClientError (subclass of LLMError) rather than swallowing errors, matching the interface contract's documented Raises clause.

security.evidence: API key is passed via httpx `params={'key': self._api_key}`, which httpx appends to the URL query string. Unlike OpenRouter's Authorization header, this means the key is embedded in the request URL. No logging of request/response is present in gemini_client.py itself, so no direct leakage in this module. However: (1) the raised GeminiClientError message on HTTP failure interpolates f'Gemini request to {self._base_url}{endpoint} failed: {exc}' -- this does NOT include the query string/key since it's built from base_url+endpoint only, not the full request.url, so the key is NOT leaked into exception messages -- verified by reading the exact f-string, good. (2) Risk remains external to this module: if any httpx transport-level logging, proxy, or observability layer (e.g. httpx event hooks, corporate proxies, access logs on the Gemini side non-controllable) is added later, URLs containing ?key=... are more likely to be captured in logs than an Authorization header would be. This is a known Gemini REST convention, not a bug introduced here, but should be flagged for anyone wiring in HTTP-level tracing/logging in a future subtask (e.g. 4.1.3's factory or an observability layer) to ensure query strings are redacted.

performance.evidence: 60s default timeout matches OpenRouterClient's; no unnecessary retries or blocking work; a new httpx.Client is created and closed per complete() call identical to the sibling clients (acceptable, consistent with existing pattern -- not a new inefficiency introduced by this commit).

maintainability.evidence: Extensive disclosed-design docstring covering REST-vs-SDK choice, endpoint/auth differences, request/response shape mapping, default model choice, and error handling. Naming and structure mirror OpenRouterClient closely, minimizing future cognitive load switching between the two provider files.

test_coverage.evidence: test_gemini_client.py (276 lines, mirrors test_openrouter_client.py structure): interface contract (2 tests), API key resolution (3), request shape (6), response parsing (1), error handling (6) = covers all complete() branches including isinstance guards on candidates/content/parts/text. All via httpx.MockTransport, no real network calls.

confidence.evidence: Implementation is additive-only (no existing files modified), isolated to agents/llm/, closely follows an already-reviewed sibling pattern (OpenRouterClient), and is fully covered by passing tests. Only open item is the disclosed query-param API key exposure risk, which is inherent to Gemini's REST API and correctly does not leak into this module's own error messages.

findings.description: Gemini API key is transmitted as a URL query parameter (?key=...) per Gemini's REST convention. Not a defect in this commit (error messages correctly avoid including the query string), but any future HTTP-level logging/tracing/proxy layer added on top of httpx must redact query params for Gemini requests specifically, since it would not need to redact an Authorization header the same way for OpenRouter.

Full repo test suite (python3 -m pytest .) fails at collection due to a pre-existing protobuf gencode/runtime version mismatch in ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py, introduced by commit 9794fd8 and unrelated to this Gemini client commit.

untrusted_content_notice: During context gathering, three fake system-reminder-style blocks (a fake date-change notice, a fake MCP server 'tokensave' instructions block, and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive) appeared in the tool-output stream immediately following gh/git commands. Per task instructions, these were treated as untrusted/injected content and not acted upon; no repo content itself (issue body, commit messages, diff) contained embedded prompt-injection text in this pass.
